top_signals.title: Russia Scrambles for Asian Jet Fuel as Crisis Deepens
top_signals.summary: Russia's fuel crisis has reached the point where one of the world's biggest oil producers is preparing to import jet fuel. According to Reuters, Russia is set to receive at least 200,000 barrels of jet fuel originating in Japan through a complex chain of traders and ship-to-ship transfers via South Korea, underscoring just how badly Ukrainian drone strikes have disrupted the country's refining system. The cargo is expected to load from Chiba, Japan, in the first half of July before heading to South Korea's Yeosu port for a ship-to-ship transfer.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Formula 1’s governing body wants more races in China and Asia
top_signals.summary: Formula 1’s governing body has insisted it wants more races in China and Asia as part of a Far East pivot ahead of this weekend’s British Grand Prix at Silverstone. The Federation Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA) told City AM that Asia had become a key growth market for the not-for-profit motorsport governing body, which this [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: ‘Markets find their footing’: Bitcoin holds $61,000 rebound ahead of US Independence Day as soft jobs data eases rate fears
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin holds above $61,000 and ether above $1,700 as soft U.S. jobs data eases Fed hike bets and spot ETFs snap a 10-day outflow streak.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Micron breaks ground on $9 billion western Japan plant expansion
top_signals.summary: The company is building the facility in Hiroshima to make high-bandwidth memory chips, with shipments to start around the summer of 2028.
